{"key_event":"Consolidation of the Portalian constitutional state","reasoning":"The 1833 order, war victory at Yungay, growing customs revenues, education institutions, and southern/Magellan assertions pushed Chile from moderate toward strong state capacity. Civil society gained some elite associational and print strength through the Generation of 1842 and new educational institutions, but managed elections, Conservative dominance, and centralized authority kept autonomous political society weak.","society_change":0.4,"society_power":3.9,"state_change":0.8,"state_power":7.3}
